title: G1: finstack balance_sheet / key_ratios omit banking fields (CASA/NIM/tier-1/GNPA/deposits/loans) — cross-market
description:
Confirmed 2026-04-22 in M0.3 smoke test on HDFCBANK.NS (India) and JPM (US). finstack-mcp 0.10.0:

balance_sheet(HDFCBANK | JPM):
  - NO total_deposits, casa_ratio, tier_1_ratio / cet1_ratio / car, gnpa, nnpa, nim, loans, allowance_for_loan_losses.
  - Returns generic US-GAAP-style bank aggregates only (cash_cash_equivalents_and_federal_funds_sold, trading_securities, held_to_maturity_securities, investments_and_advances).

key_ratios(HDFCBANK | JPM):
  - Same generic shape as non-bank tickers (pe, pb, p/s, roe, roa, margins).
  - NO NIM / CASA / GNPA / tier-1 / CET-1 / CAR substitutes.
  - gross_margin and ebitda_margin = 0 (meaningless for a bank).
  - debt_to_equity / current_ratio / quick_ratio / free_cash_flow silently null for banks.

income_statement is bank-flavoured and OK (interest_income / interest_expense / net_interest_income / salaries / occupancy are present) — only balance_sheet and key_ratios are gap surfaces.

Evidence: research/mcp-smoke-test-india.md §2 C08/C11, research/mcp-smoke-test-us.md §2 C08/C11. Canonical matrix row: research/mcp-gaps.md G1.

Downstream impact: M3 banking_nbfc scheme fixture cannot be sourced from finstack. SPEC §15.3 allows hand-curated fixtures (e.g., tests/scoring-engine/hdfcbank.json seeded from Screener.in). Phase 2 bank analytics also downgraded until M3 decides fixture source.

title: Holdings-only mode: redesign SPEC and implementation.md
description:
The user has chosen holdings-only maintenance in Wealthfolio (no transactions, dividends, or corp-action entries). This invalidates several SPEC assumptions and collapses the core design premise of the corp-actions-monitor skill (M5). Edit SPEC.md and implementation.md before M5 starts; can run in parallel with M0.1 since the schema dump is mode-independent.

Decided 2026-04-21 in M0.1 planning conversation. Alternatives considered: mixed mode (log only SPLIT+DIVIDEND) and transactions-eventually; both rejected on maintenance-ergonomics grounds.
design:
## docs/SPEC.md edits

- §2 bullet 1: soften. Positions first; transactions/dividends/corp-actions described as optional, user-driven enrichment.
- §5 (Wealthfolio boundary): add 'Holdings-only mode' subsection listing which downstream features degrade (M5 semantics, long-window TWR) vs remain fully functional (research pipeline, scoring, personas, reports, our own ledger).
- §5.3: note that list-transactions-since, list-dividends-since, list-splits-since are allowed to return empty; callers MUST tolerate this and not error.
- §6.2 corp-actions-monitor: rewrite. With no activities baseline, all MCP-detected events default to 'info' severity. The skill shifts from reconciliation to informational digest. §7.3 activity-type mapping stays as guidance for user manual entry.
- §12 benchmarking: add caveat that Wealthfolio native TWR is transaction-derived. In holdings-only mode, long windows emit 'insufficient history' until daily_account_valuation snapshots accumulate. Graceful-degradation output shape specified.
- §13 corp-actions behavior: same rewrite as §6.2. Remove the 'unrecorded event' severity ladder; replace with informational classification.

## docs/implementation.md edits

- M5 deliverables + acceptance: redesign as informational digest. Drop the 'IEX 1:1 bonus unrecorded' load-bearing fixture. New acceptance: tool lists upcoming + past-90-day events with sources; never emits high-severity 'unrecorded' warnings unless activities table is non-empty.
- M6: add graceful-degradation acceptance for windows exceeding snapshot history.
- M8 Phase 1 step 7: tolerate empty transactions-snapshot.csv; don't fail the phase.
- M2: list-transactions-since etc. must return empty-but-valid output when activities is empty, not error.
acceptance_criteria: docs/SPEC.md §2, §5, §5.3, §6.2, §12, §13 reflect holdings-only as the supported maintenance mode. docs/implementation.md M2, M5, M6, M8 acceptance criteria updated accordingly. A grep for 'activities table' in SPEC surfaces either a conditional clause or a note that the table may be empty. M5 no longer has any acceptance test that presumes recorded activities.
notes: Execute before M5 starts. Can run in parallel with M0.1. Once this lands, config/wealthfolio.md (in M0.1) should reference the holdings-only maintenance guidance.
close_reason: Implemented. SPEC.md §5.2 holdings-only declaration added; §6.2/§13 rewritten as informational feed (no severity); §7.3 activity-type mapping deleted with §7.4-§7.7 renumbered to §7.3-§7.6; §10.2 phase-1 step 7 removed; §14.2 TWR retargeted to daily_account_valuation; §17 directory layout cleaned; §18.1/§18.4 skill contracts updated. implementation.md M2/M5/M6/M8 updated accordingly. Follow-up bead edits (M5/M6/M8 descriptions, M2→M5 edge drop) applied. Plan files deleted.

description:

See docs/implementation.md section M0 for the full deliverables list.

title: M2.11: M2 acceptance pass
description: Run the full acceptance suite end-to-end and record results in the close note. Steps: (1) bin/run-skill tests/wealthfolio_query/run-all.sh returns 0 green across fixture rebuild + readonly + schema + all subcommand tests. (2) wealthfolio-query export-snapshot --market india --scope-type market --scope-value india --theses input/india/theses.yaml --output /tmp/snap.csv — verify CSV first line byte-matches SPEC §7.1.1 exactly (14 columns). (3) Verify the exit-code matrix per investigation §11.1: bogus subcommand → 1, WEALTHFOLIO_DB unset → 2, --scope-value bogus --strict-scope → 2, get-avg-cost UNKNOWN.NS → 2. (4) Write-blocked proof: hand-inject an UPDATE via sqlite3 -readonly against the fixture, confirm non-zero exit + 'readonly' in stderr. (5) Record acceptance bundle (command + stdout + exit code) in the bd close note. Any failure here loops back to the owning subtask bead; this is gating only. No code changes.
design: Inline plan — gating/verification task, no new code. If any step fails, open (or reopen) the owning bead with the failure details and re-run after fix. Blocks the M2 epic close.
acceptance_criteria: All three M2 epic-level acceptance criteria met and evidence captured: CSV header byte-match, write-blocked proof, and every -- name: slug has a passing subcommand test. run-all.sh exit 0. Exit-code matrix verified. Close note contains command transcripts.
notes:
Source: research/milestones/M2-investigation.md §12 (test coverage) + §13 (acceptance bar). This task is the final gate before the epic closes, but M2.12 (deferred user-data verify) still has to clear before the epic formally closes.

Cross-doc consistency check (folded in from M2.2 close note 2026-04-30): SPEC §10 line 860 currently reads 'The command should also print or preview the exported rows for user review' for the export-snapshot Phase 1 walkthrough — which uses --output. This contradicts SPEC §6.8 (post-M2.2) saying the wrapper must NOT echo preview rows when --output is set. Reconcile §10 line 860 to either drop the 'print or preview' clause or re-scope it to the no-output path. Acceptance pass should grep 'print or preview' across docs/ to catch any sibling drift.

title: M2.10: skills/wealthfolio_query/README.md
description: Author skills/wealthfolio_query/README.md covering: (1) invocation — native (./query.sh subcmd ...) and container (bin/run-skill skills/wealthfolio_query/query.sh subcmd ...); (2) contract — six subcommands with one-line signatures matching SPEC §18.1, output shapes per investigation §9.1 (scalar bare numbers for cash/net-worth/avg-cost; JSON for TWR; CSV or JSON for list-holdings and export-snapshot); (3) failure modes — exit codes 0/1/2/3 per investigation §11.1, stderr format 'wealthfolio-query: <message>'; (4) env-var setup — WEALTHFOLIO_DB (container-side) and WEALTHFOLIO_DB_DIR (host-side) per M2.1; (5) concurrency — parallel readers safe under sqlite3 -readonly; (6) read-only guarantees — the three layers of defense (no DDL in SQL, RO open modes, no string-formatted SQL); (7) cross-links to config/wealthfolio.md and the fixture-based test harness. Written after M2.4 exists so signatures are accurate; references the specific test files M2.5–M2.9 land.

acceptance_criteria: README covers all six subcommands with correct signatures and output shapes; exit-code matrix documented; env-var setup lines up with docker-compose.yml after M2.1; read-only defense layers explicit; links to config/wealthfolio.md and the test harness present.
notes: Source: research/milestones/M2-investigation.md §11 operational contract + §13.1 deliverables. Can start once M2.4 lands but is most useful with M2.6/M2.7/M2.8/M2.9 complete so examples match reality.

title: M2.8: get-portfolio-twr + chained product + forward-fill
description: Implement the TWR subcommand: M6's benchmark assembler consumes it. (1) Create skills/wealthfolio_query/portfolio_twr.py — runs the get-portfolio-twr named query (reads account_id/valuation_date/total_value_base/cumulative_net_contribution_base from daily_account_valuation, filtered by --market via the accounts join), forward-fills missing calendar days with V[prev] and C=0 (no-op factor of 1 in the product — handles both Wealthfolio population regimes identically), computes daily returns r[t] = (V[t] - V[t-1] - C[t]) / V[t-1] where C[t] = cumulative_net_contribution_base[t] - cumulative_net_contribution_base[t-1], chains TWR = ∏(1 + r[t]) - 1 over the half-open window (close of :start exclusive, close of :end inclusive). Endpoint convention: --start = --end returns {"twr": 0.0} with empty series. (2) Output JSON: {twr, start, end, series:[{date, V, C, r}, ...]}. (3) query.sh exec's into this helper. (4) tests/wealthfolio_query/test_twr.sh seeds a known V/C series in the fixture, asserts chained TWR matches a hand-computed expected value within 1bp tolerance; also asserts --start = --end yields TWR 0.0 exactly.
acceptance_criteria: get-portfolio-twr --market india --start 2026-03-30 --end 2026-04-30 emits the documented JSON shape with twr/start/end/series fields; chained product matches hand-computed expected to within 1bp on the fixture; zero-day window (--start = --end) returns TWR 0.0 and empty series; forward-fill behaves correctly on a fixture seeded with gap days; CAST(... AS REAL) precision sufficient — no Python Decimal needed.
notes: Plan written: plans/M2/M2.8-get-portfolio-twr.md

title: M2.9: --strict-scope flag + test_strict_scope.sh
description: Add the --strict-scope flag to the three subcommands that take --scope-type/--scope-value: export-snapshot, list-holdings, get-cash-balance. When --strict-scope is set, the wrapper issues a small validation query before the main query (confirms --scope-value market ∈ {india, us}; for account_group/account confirms at least one active accounts row with the matching column) and exits 2 with a useful stderr if no rows match. Without --strict-scope, empty scope stays silent (exit 0, empty/0.0 output) — preserving existing M2.6/M2.7 behavior. Passing --strict-scope to get-net-worth / get-avg-cost / get-portfolio-twr is a usage error (exit 1) with stderr explaining the flag only applies to scoped subcommands. Add tests/wealthfolio_query/test_strict_scope.sh: asserts --scope-value bogus-account-name --strict-scope exits 2 with 'readonly'-free stderr containing 'scope'; asserts --scope-value india --strict-scope on the india market exits 0; asserts --strict-scope on a non-scoped subcommand exits 1.
design: Inline plan — the flag is a thin validation pre-query shared across three subcommands. Implement once in query.sh (or query.py for export-snapshot) and call from each. Subcommand-applicability table in investigation §6 is authoritative.
acceptance_criteria: --strict-scope --scope-value typoaccount on list-holdings exits 2 with clear stderr; --strict-scope on get-net-worth exits 1 (usage error); test_strict_scope.sh passes; non-strict behavior of M2.6/M2.7 unchanged (empty scope still exits 0 when --strict-scope is absent).
notes: Source: research/milestones/M2-investigation.md §6 + §12 test coverage. M8's Phase 1 prompt uses --strict-scope so typos fail loud; lower-level tooling omits the flag when empty-result is valid.

title: M2.7: export-snapshot + theses merge + byte-identical re-run
description: Implement the export-snapshot subcommand: the one that Phase 1 of the agent loop depends on. (1) Create skills/wealthfolio_query/export_snapshot.py — runs the export-snapshot named query, reads input/{market}/theses.yaml, joins thesis by canonical Yahoo ticker (missing thesis = empty string, never an error per SPEC §19.2 invariant 13), writes CSV via csv.writer with QUOTE_MINIMAL so thesis bodies with commas/quotes/newlines escape correctly. (2) query.sh exec's into this helper for export-snapshot. (3) Named query in skills/sql/wealthfolio-queries.sql ends with ORDER BY ticker, account so re-runs against the same DB state are byte-identical. (4) --theses auto-resolves from --market when omitted. (5) When --output is set the wrapper writes the file and exits silently — no preview echo, per SPEC §6.8 narrowing landed in M2.2. (6) Create input/us/theses.yaml with the same shape as input/india/theses.yaml (empty strings for the MVP US roster). (7) tests/wealthfolio_query/test_export_snapshot.sh asserts: CSV header byte-matches SPEC §7.1.1 (14 columns in exact order); allocation_pct column sums to ~1.0; two consecutive runs produce byte-identical output (diff -q).
acceptance_criteria: export-snapshot --market india --scope-type market --scope-value india --theses input/india/theses.yaml --output /tmp/snap.csv produces a CSV whose first line matches 'ticker,name,market,currency,account,account_group,asset_type,quantity,avg_cost,snapshot_price,snapshot_market_value,allocation_pct,unrealized_pl_pct,thesis' exactly; two consecutive runs against the fixture yield byte-identical files; input/us/theses.yaml exists with the MVP US roster; thesis bodies containing commas are quoted correctly; missing thesis emits empty string with no warning noise.
notes: Plan written: plans/M2/M2.7-export-snapshot.md

title: M2.6: Four scalar/list subcommands (list-holdings, get-cash-balance, get-net-worth, get-avg-cost)
description: Implement the four shell-only subcommands against the skeleton in M2.4 and the fixture in M2.5. (1) list-holdings: rows with the 13 non-thesis columns from SPEC §7.1.1, filters --market and --scope-type/--scope-value, ORDER BY ticker, account; supports --format csv|json (default json). (2) get-cash-balance: reads daily_account_valuation.cash_balance_base for the most recent valuation_date per scoped account, summed; --currency omitted=base sum, INR/USD selects accounts.currency=:ccy and reads cash_balance native-currency column; empty scope returns 0.0 silently. (3) get-net-worth: sums daily_account_valuation.total_value_base across active accounts in the requested market; --date defaults to most recent valuation_date (not calendar today); --currency same semantics as cash-balance; no --scope-type. (4) get-avg-cost <ticker>: quantity-weighted SUM(qty*avg_cost)/SUM(qty) across all active lots from holdings_snapshots.positions using the JSON paths pinned in M2.3; ticker-not-held exits 2 with 'ticker not in active holdings' message. Scalar subcommands return bare numbers (e.g. '1234567.89\n'), not JSON objects. Add one test_*.sh per subcommand under tests/wealthfolio_query/ with fixture-backed expected values.
acceptance_criteria: Each of the four subcommands produces the output shape documented in investigation §9.1 against the M2.5 fixture; test_list_holdings.sh, test_cash_balance.sh, test_net_worth.sh, test_avg_cost.sh all pass; get-avg-cost on a non-held ticker exits 2; get-cash-balance on an empty scope without --strict-scope exits 0 with '0.0' stdout; scalar returns are bare numbers (no JSON wrapper) by default; rows and number formats are stable across two consecutive runs.
notes: Plan written: plans/M2/M2.6-scalar-list-subcommands.md

title: M2.5: Test harness (fixture.sql + build_fixture.sh + run-all.sh + readonly/schema tests)
description: Create tests/wealthfolio_query/ with the committed fixture infrastructure that every subcommand test later reuses. Files: fixture.sql (DDL copy of research/wealthfolio-schema-v3.2.1.txt + INSERTs seeding 3 accounts [1 INR brokerage, 1 INR PMS group, 1 USD US brokerage], 5 India + 3 US EQUITY assets across XNSE/XBOM/XNAS MICs plus one MIC-less asset to exercise display_code fallback, 1 ETF NIFTYBEES.NS, 2 cash-only positions, holdings_snapshots rows with realistic positions JSON using the paths pinned in M2.3, quotes for every asset with a 60-day history on one ticker, daily_account_valuation rows covering 90+ days for two accounts); build_fixture.sh (rm -f fixture.db && sqlite3 fixture.db < fixture.sql); run-all.sh (invokes every test_*.sh in order, fails non-zero on any); test_readonly.sh (runs sqlite3 -readonly fixture.db 'UPDATE accounts SET name="x"', asserts non-zero exit + 'readonly' in stderr, also asserts grep -cE '^\s*(CREATE|INSERT|UPDATE|DELETE|DROP|ALTER)\b' on skills/sql/wealthfolio-queries.sql returns 0); test_schema.sh (diffs .schema fixture.db against research/wealthfolio-schema-v3.2.1.txt less row data, fails on drift). Add a make test-wealthfolio-query target that cleans, rebuilds, and runs.
acceptance_criteria: make test-wealthfolio-query from a clean tree exits 0; fixture.db is rebuilt from fixture.sql on every invocation and is not committed to git (only fixture.sql is); test_readonly.sh and test_schema.sh both pass; run-all.sh returns non-zero if any sub-test fails.
notes:
Source: research/milestones/M2-investigation.md §3 (fixture strategy), §8 (read-only layers), §12 (test coverage), §9 schema-check-in-tests-only. Plan file: plans/M2/M2.5-test-harness.md (created when claimed). Committed .sql not .db keeps the fixture auditable and sub-100ms to rebuild.
Plan written: plans/M2/M2.5-test-harness.md

title: M2.4: Wrapper skeleton (query.sh + query.py + named-query loader)
description: Create the skills/wealthfolio_query/ package with the dispatch surface that every subcommand bead (M2.6/M2.7/M2.8) plugs into. Files: __init__.py (empty package marker); query.py (load_query(slug) that re-parses skills/sql/wealthfolio-queries.sql by splitting on ^-- name: , exposes execute(slug, params) using stdlib sqlite3.connect(f'file:{path}?mode=ro&immutable=1', uri=True)); query.sh (bash, set -euo pipefail, arg parser, six subcommand dispatcher that exec's into python3 -m skills.wealthfolio_query.query for export-snapshot/get-portfolio-twr and runs sqlite3 -readonly directly for the four scalar/list subcommands). No subcommand bodies in this bead — just the skeleton + dispatch + RO-open plumbing + help. Shell escaping for .parameter set is single-quoted explicit.
acceptance_criteria: skills/wealthfolio_query/{__init__.py, query.py, query.sh} exist; query.sh --help prints the signature block; query.sh with an unknown subcommand exits 1; query.sh with WEALTHFOLIO_DB unset exits 2 with a clear message; query.py's load_query() round-trips every -- name: slug in skills/sql/wealthfolio-queries.sql; attempting to execute a hand-crafted UPDATE through the RO-open path raises sqlite3.OperationalError with 'readonly' in the message. notes:

title: M2.1: Docker mount + env-var contract reconcile
description: Fix docker-compose.yml so the Wealthfolio DB bind-mount actually works: (1) switch the bind source from the stale ~/Library/Application Support/Wealthfolio/ledger.db path to the directory ~/Library/Application Support/com.teymz.wealthfolio, (2) change from a file mount to a directory mount so SQLite WAL sidecars (app.db-wal, app.db-shm) are visible, (3) collapse the host-side WEALTHFOLIO_DB_PATH vs container-side WEALTHFOLIO_DB muddle into a single host var WEALTHFOLIO_DB_DIR with WEALTHFOLIO_DB=/wealthfolio/app.db inside the container, (4) default WEALTHFOLIO_DB_DIR to ${HOME}/Library/Application Support/com.teymz.wealthfolio for portability (no hardcoded /Users/mrihal/...). Optional: add a one-line pointer in README.md cross-linking config/wealthfolio.md. read_only:true preserved per SPEC §19.2 invariant 11.
design: Inline plan — single file edit (docker-compose.yml). See research/milestones/M2-investigation.md §7 for the exact YAML shape. No Python/shell plumbing. Confirm sqlite3 can open the mounted app.db inside the container before closing.
acceptance_criteria: docker-compose.yml uses a directory bind-mount of ${WEALTHFOLIO_DB_DIR:-${HOME}/Library/Application Support/com.teymz.wealthfolio} to /wealthfolio (read_only); env block sets WEALTHFOLIO_DB=/wealthfolio/app.db; bin/run-skill skills/wealthfolio_query/query.sh --help (or equivalent trivial read) succeeds against the mounted DB; config/wealthfolio.md env-var section still matches compose reality.

title: M1.10: shellcheck + final acceptance
description: Run shellcheck skills/scripts/*.sh (including _claim-common.sh), fix any violations. title: G7: nsekit corporate_actions(period='3M') under-reports events
description:
Confirmed 2026-04-22 in M0.3 India smoke test. nsekit-mcp 0.0.22:

corporate_actions(RELIANCE, period="3M") -> empty (genuinely: no events in window; 1Y probe returned 1 event).
corporate_actions(HDFCBANK, period="3M") -> empty.
  BUT: nsekit equity_price_history(HDFCBANK, "1Y") shows a large price step on 19-Mar-2026 (prev_close 843 -> open 770, close 798.2) consistent with an ex-date event within the 3M window that should have been reported.
corporate_actions(INFY, period="3M") -> empty.

Evidence: research/mcp-smoke-test-india.md §2 C22/C23/C24 + price evidence C20. Canonical matrix row: research/mcp-gaps.md G7.

Hypothesis: either the "3M" enum is interpreted as a shorter window than 90 days upstream, or the event feed genuinely does not include HDFCBANK's recent event. M5 should:
  1. Re-probe with explicit start_date / end_date (today -90d .. today +14d) rather than the period enum.
  2. If still empty, fall back to Yahoo `actions` per SPEC §6.2 / ai-portfolio-manager-ap0 (holdings-only semantics already allow Yahoo-fallback with source attribution).

Downstream impact: M5 corp-actions-monitor is informational only (ap0 decision) — this is not a correctness risk, only a source-coverage risk. Acceptable for Phase 1 with Yahoo fallback. Revisit if NseKit ships a fix before M5 starts.

title: G9: finstack sec_filing multi-year lookback returns fewer filings than requested for JPM
description:
Confirmed 2026-04-22 in M0.3 US smoke test. finstack-mcp 0.10.0:

sec_filing(JPM, filing_type="10-K", count=5):
  - Returns count=1. Only latest 10-K (filed 2026-02-13 for FY2025).
  - Prior FY2024/2023/2022/2021 10-Ks exist on EDGAR but are not returned.

sec_filing(JPM, filing_type="10-Q", count=5):
  - Returns count=3. Only 2025 quarters (Q1/Q2/Q3). Older 10-Qs absent.

Contrast:
  - sec_filing(AAPL, 10-K, count=5) -> 5 filings returned (2021-10-29 through 2025-10-31).
  - sec_filing(MSFT, 10-K, count=5) -> 5 filings returned.
  - sec_filing(AAPL, 10-Q, count=5) -> 5 filings.
  - sec_filing(MSFT, 10-Q, count=5) -> 5 filings.

Tool signature (symbol, filing_type, count) matches SPEC §18.2 — only the ticker-level lookback depth is off, specifically for JPM. May affect other large-bank filers (BAC, WFC, C) — untested.

Evidence: research/mcp-smoke-test-us.md §2 C14/C17. Canonical matrix row: research/mcp-gaps.md G9.

Downstream impact (M9): Phase 2 US Tier-1 most-recent-filing flow is NOT blocked (latest 10-K/10-Q is returned). Multi-year 10-K diffing / year-over-year filing analytics requires a fallback: direct EDGAR `browse-edgar?CIK=<cik>&type=10-K` call to enumerate prior filings when finstack returns count < requested.

title: G4: finstack routes Indian ADR-listed tickers (INFY etc.) to US ADR in USD, not NSE INR
description:
Confirmed 2026-04-22 in M0.3 India smoke test. finstack-mcp 0.10.0:

For INFY (NSE-listed, also NYSE ADR-listed):
- company_profile(INFY) -> exchange="NYQ", currency="USD", market_cap in USD (~55.56B), not NSE/INR.
- income_statement(INFY) -> currency="USD".
- balance_sheet(INFY) -> currency="USD".
- key_ratios(INFY) -> currency="USD".

RELIANCE and HDFCBANK return exchange="NSI" and currency="INR" correctly (they are not ADR-listed).

nsekit equity_price_history(INFY, "1Y") does return NSE INR price data — viable fallback path for INFY price/quote data.

Evidence: research/mcp-smoke-test-india.md §2 C03/C06/C09/C12/C21. Canonical matrix row: research/mcp-gaps.md G4.

Downstream impact (M9 fundamentals-fetch aggregator): when an Indian ticker is ADR-listed, finstack returns US ADR financials in USD which do not match the SPEC §18.2 India shape (INR, Indian AS). Aggregator must either:
  (a) detect ADR routing and fall back to Tier-B / Yahoo / web for fundamentals (SPEC §15), or
  (b) convert USD figures to INR at as_of FX and flag the data provenance in sources[] / missing_fields.

Recommended: (a). ADR detection heuristic: finstack company_profile(symbol).exchange == "NYQ" AND country == "India" => ADR routing. Known ADR-listed Indian names to consider: INFY, WIT (Wipro), HDB (HDFC Bank NYSE ADR), IBN (ICICI Bank ADR), RDY (Dr. Reddy's), TTM (Tata Motors).

title: M6: benchmark
description:
Goal: Alpha numbers per standard window are computable programmatically for India and US portfolios.

Dependencies: M0, M2 (TWR source).

See docs/implementation.md section M6 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- Fixture portfolio produces an alpha that matches the hand-computed value within 1 basis point.
- Running twice with the same inputs produces identical output (determinism modulo Yahoo price revisions; in tests, prices are stubbed).
- Markdown output contains the phrase 'price-return (no dividend reinvestment)' adjacent to ^NSEI rows.
- When the requested window exceeds available daily_account_valuation history, output marks that window `insufficient_history` and the overall exit code remains 0.
notes:
Risks:
- Yahoo rate limits and occasional ticker outages. Mitigation: cache raw price arrays under temp/research/ for the run so retries are free.
- Portfolio TWR is derived from Wealthfolio's daily_account_valuation snapshots as the sole source; there is no transaction-derived fallback. If a requested window lacks sufficient snapshot history, that window degrades to insufficient_history rather than failing the run.
